summary: Petrochina L5E89 PP yarn cut 85 USD to 1,000 USD/ton CIF
NE Asia PP fell 90-110 USD/ton per non-Platts assessments
paras: July 3, offer prices for PP from domestic producers continued to be cut and competition is very strong.

In the middle of this week, in the south, Hyosung Vina's PP yarn offers were commonly at 31,500–31,600 VND/kg incl. VAT. Today, Hyosung Vina's F501 PP yarn offer dropped sharply to 30,300 VND/kg incl. VAT for prompt delivery.

Similarly, at the start of this week, BSR's PP T3034 offer at the Quang Ngai warehouse was 30,400 VND/kg incl. VAT, the offer into HCMC was 31,000 VND/kg incl. VAT, and to the north 31,200 VND/kg incl. VAT. Today, some distributors cut this product to 30,700–30,800 VND/kg incl. VAT in HCMC, down 200–300 VND/kg from the start of the week.

For imported PP homo, southern-market offers are commonly 31,500–33,000 VND/kg incl. VAT. For example, Jinneng China's HP550J PP yarn was offered by a leading major distributor at 31,400 VND/kg incl. VAT…

Except for LSP, which has sold out, Vietnam's remaining PP plants are still running at maximum capacity. The NSRP plant has just opened a tender to sell 7,000 tons of PP, with delivery from July 7–20, 2026. The volume may rise or fall within a 20% range at NSRP's discretion.

In the north, yesterday a major NSRP distributor lowered its PP 114G offer to 30,500 VND/kg incl. VAT and delivery, down 500 VND/kg from the start of the week. Some manufacturing customers bid for this product at 30,000 VND/kg incl. VAT for July delivery; the number of customers bidding and closing purchases was not large.

Many traders are still trying to keep PP offers high, with PP homo offered in the 30,500–32,000 VND/kg incl. VAT range; however, in reality in the north, the common transaction level for PP yarn is currently only around 30,000 VND/kg incl. VAT…
PP copo in the north is commonly offered by traders at 34,000–36,000 VND/kg incl. VAT; some products recorded transactions at 33,000 VND/kg incl. VAT…
On imports, today a distributor offered Petrochina's L5E89 PP yarn down sharply to 1,000 USD/ton CIF, down 85 USD/ton from the previous week…

Across the region, per Platts' assessments, in Northeast Asia the price level for PP yarn/injection and IPP Film fell only slightly by 5 USD/ton this week, while BOPP fell 15 USD/ton. In Southeast Asia, PP yarn/injection fell 50 USD/ton to 1,045 USD/ton CFR, BOPP fell 50 USD/ton to 1,065 USD/ton CFR, IPP Film fell 50 USD/ton to 1,095 USD/ton CFR, while PP block copolymer fell sharply by 100 USD/ton to 1,045 USD/ton CFR, level with the PP homo price.

Per assessments from another international organization, PP grades in Northeast Asia fell sharply by 90–110 USD/ton this week; in Southeast Asia, PP grades fell sharply by 100–120 USD/ton, with PP block copo down 120 USD/ton at the low end but 60 USD/ton at the high end; in India and other South Asian countries, PP grades fell 30–60 USD/ton…

Thus, PP prices in Asia continued to fall sharply this week, impacted by the declining price of the input feedstock propylene. PP export offers from China are fairly limited because China's FOB prices are higher than levels in other major export markets such as Southeast Asia. Abundant supply and weak demand are forcing many suppliers to cut prices continuously to compete.

summary: US Cynpol LDPE Film closed lowest at 1,100-1,130 USD/ton CIF
paras: After a mid-week pause, in today's end-of-week trading session on July 3, the domestic PE market again saw many sellers cutting prices sharply, especially for far-forward cargoes delivering in September–October 2026.

For example, in the north, many sellers lowered offers for Qamar's LLDPE Film FD21HS and Saudi Sabic's 218WJ to 30,400–30,700 VND/kg incl. VAT, down 700–1,000 VND/kg from mid-week. One distributor offered Qamar's LLDPE Film FD21HS for September 2026 delivery at a much lower 27,100–27,200 VND/kg incl. VAT…

For LDPE, in the north, some sellers lowered offers for certain US- and China-origin LDPE Film products to 38,000 VND/kg incl. VAT; offers for Middle East-origin LDPE Film such as Sabic's 4024WN, Tasnee's 4025AS/1925AS and Borouge's FT6230 also only hovered around 40,000–41,000 VND/kg incl. VAT; however, Lotte Titan's LDPE Film 260GG was still offered at 42,000–43,000 VND/kg incl. VAT due to limited prompt-delivery inventory. In the south, PTT Thailand's LDPE 2426K was offered down to 40,000–40,500 VND/kg incl. VAT…
For HDPE, market offer prices saw little further movement, as many products do not have overly abundant prompt-delivery inventory. HDPE grades commonly ranged between 33,000–36,000 VND/kg incl. VAT.

On imports, today a distributor offered Petrochina China's LLDPE Film 7042N (with additives) into Vietnam at 960 USD/ton CIF, down 60 USD/ton from the previous week; Petrochina's HDPE yarn was offered at a fairly low 1,000 USD/ton CIF.

One distributor offered STL China's HDPE Film FS7000 at 1,070–1,080 USD/ton CIF but received no response, as the market had already recorded closed HDPE Film deals from PTT Thailand at 1,000 USD/ton CIF and Lotte's product at 1,030 USD/ton CIF… Some sources say the lowest closed price for HDPE Film is currently 960 USD/ton CIF.

Meanwhile, the lowest closed import price for LDPE Film is US Cynpol's product, closed at 1,100–1,130 USD/ton CIF depending on grade. Asian-origin LDPE Film is being offered at 1,180–1,300 USD/ton CIF…

Across the region, per Platts' assessments, in Northeast Asia the price level for HDPE Film/yarn/injection/blow fell 25 USD/ton from the previous week and LLDPE fell 25 USD/ton, while LDPE rose slightly by 5 USD/ton after a sharp drop the prior week. In Southeast Asia, HDPE grades fell 30 USD/ton this week to 1,150–1,160 USD/ton CFR; LLDPE fell 20 USD/ton to 1,150 USD/ton CFR; LLDPE metallocene C6 fell 20 USD/ton to 1,300 USD/ton CFR; conversely LDPE rose 30 USD/ton to 1,450 USD/ton CFR…

Overall, the downtrend in the Asian PE market continued this week, driven by falling input feedstock prices and weak downstream demand that restrained negotiations. In Southeast Asia and Vietnam, very few transactions were done as many buyers stayed cautious amid weakness across the entire PE supply chain.

summary: SE Asia PVC fell 25 USD to 700 USD/ton CFR (Platts)
Weak demand, falling ethylene and freight keep pressure on
Coal SG-5 20,500 (North); AGC Vina PM66R 20,900 (South) VND/kg
paras: Per Platts' assessments, the price level for PVC in China fell slightly by 5 USD/ton from the previous week; however, prices in Southeast Asia fell 25 USD/ton to 700 USD/ton CFR.

Per assessments from another international organization, PVC in Northeast Asia was flat versus the previous week; prices in Southeast Asia fell 10 USD/ton to 690–730 USD/ton CFR; prices in India fell 20 USD/ton to 700–740 USD/ton CFR…
PVC prices in Asia fell slightly during the week under pressure from weak demand, softening market sentiment, and a falling price of the input feedstock ethylene.

According to market participants, the Southeast Asian import market faced added pressure from lower offers for China-origin goods, average demand, and a downward trend in freight rates. In South Asia, India extended the customs duty exemption for certain imported petrochemical and chemical products, including PVC, from June 30 to July 15, helping keep the market relatively stable.

In Vietnam, after the drop at the start of this week, PVC sellers are holding prices more cautiously. China-origin coal-based PVC SG-5 remains the lowest at 20,500 VND/kg incl. VAT in the north. Although this is the market's lowest price, the gap versus oil-based product has narrowed to only about 500–1,000 VND/kg, so it is also not attractive to buyers.

Notably in the south, oil-based PVC PM66R from domestic producer AGC Vina is lowest at 20,900 VND/kg incl. VAT, creating very strong competitive pressure. Shintech US-origin PVC SE950/SE1000 was offered by a major distributor at 21,500 VND/kg incl. VAT, notably below the general price level of the previous week…

bullets: Petrochina L5E89 PP yarn cut 85 USD to 1,000 USD/ton CIF
NE Asia PP fell 90-110 USD/ton per non-Platts assessments
US Cynpol LDPE Film closed lowest at 1,100-1,130 USD/ton CIF
SE Asia PVC fell 25 USD to 700 USD/ton CFR (Platts)
Weak demand, falling ethylene and freight keep pressure on
Coal SG-5 20,500 (North); AGC Vina PM66R 20,900 (South) VND/kg
